stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Some powerful works of literature have prompted lawmakers to pass new legislation.
  • Twelve Years a Slave (1853) is the memoir of Solomon Northup, who was kidnapped and enslaved in Louisiana.
  • Northup's book strengthened public support for the legal reforms that culminated in the Thirteenth Amendment.
  • Silent Spring (1962) is Rachel Carson's nonfiction critique of agricultural pesticide use.
  • Carson's book contributed to the passage of the 1972 Federal Environmental Pesticide Control Act.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two books. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: They may have addressed different topics, but Northup and Carson both influenced readers.
The Thirteenth Amendment resulted in part from a book by Northup, while the 1972 Federal Environmental Pesticide Control Act resulted in part from a book by Carson.
Although both are powerful works of literature that contributed to new legislation, Northup's book is a personal memoir, while Carson's is a work of investigative nonfiction.
Twelve Years a Slave and Silent Spring are two works of literature that contributed to new legislation (concerning slavery and pesticides, respectively).

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer because it explicitly identifies a difference between the books—memoir versus investigative nonfiction—after noting their shared trait.

Choices A, B, and D all emphasize similarities rather than a difference.
